title: sinapis cheiranthos subsp. montana
intro: Sinapis cheiranthos subsp. montana, also known as alpine mustard or mountain mustard, is a subspecies of mustard plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture, infusion, or poultice, and it is also used in traditional cooking and as a spice, adding a pungent flavor to various dishes.
